new_jobs.title: Managing Director
new_jobs.description:
**Benefits:**
* Free food \& snacks
* Health insurance
* Opportunity for advancement
* Paid time off
* 401(k) matching
* Competitive salary
* Dental insurance
* Flexible schedule
**Managing Director (HI \& CA)**
================================

**Type:** Full\-time
**Experience:** Senior level
**Functions:** Executive, Accounting, Tax, Financial Management, Management
**Reports to:** President/CEO, HiGroup
**Compensation:** Salary
**FLSA Status:** Exempt

  

**COMPANY DESCRIPTION**  


Hawaii Accounting LLC (HiAccounting) and California Accounting \& Tax, Inc. (CaAccounting) are seeking a **Managing Director** to lead our team of accounting and tax professionals in Hawaii and California. HiAccounting and CaAccounting are affiliates of The Hawaii Group, Hawaii’s leading business process outsourcing firm. The ideal Managing Director is team, client and community focused. A strong leader with the ability to bring cohesiveness in culture, processes and client service across multiple offices in Hawaii and California.  

Our team of accounting and tax professionals work together to serve businesses, nonprofits, and individuals on their outsourced accounting, bookkeeping, tax preparation, tax planning, and payroll service needs. We strongly believe in working personally and directly with our clients, connecting through our core principles of integrity, transparency, and dedicated client service.  

HiAccounting’s primary location is in Honolulu with additional offices in Maui and Big Island. CaAccounting has a location inLakeport. We are a thriving company with a focus on transformational growth in 2026\-27!

**POSITION SUMMARY**
HiAccounting is seeking a Managing Director to oversee the accounting and tax departments in Hawaii and California. The Managing Director is primarily responsible for overseeing the day\-to\-day operations and oversight of the team of 15 staff in Hawaii and 8 staff in California. This is a high\-level position that works closely with the CEO/President, Chief Financial Officer and Director of HR. As part of the executive leadership team, the Managing Director is essential to the implementation of our company’s vision and success of the organization.  

The Managing Director reports to the President/CEO of HiGroup.  

The Managing Director may also be requested, on an as needed basis, to help fulfill duties, align processes, management staff or assist with other functions for the management of related or sister companies such as but not limited to DemandHR.  

**ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS**
**Client Service**
* Develop and maintain strong relationships with business owners and CEOs of current and potential clients
* Effectively manage client relationships to enhance client satisfaction
* Consult, work with, and service our client base to make recommendations on business improvement and process improvement and serve as a business advisor to client
* Identify, formalize, and execute best practices in onboarding, implementation and project management in order to meet client accounting and tax needs

**Business Development**
* Lead team on proposals and business development calls
* Works closely with sales and marketing to maintain and develop ideas for advertising such as but not limited to: content marketing, website, social media, blogging, networking, and other programs to enhance growth.
* Execute our practice's growth strategy by helping lead our expansion into new service areas

 **Team and Office Management**
* Align processes, cultural values and team management for Hawaii and California
* Maximize revenue from each client and ensure clients are retained; identify cross\-sell and up\-sell opportunities
* Effectively manage the fundamentals of billings, billing rates, collections and work in progress (i.e. realization, utilization, leverage, accounts receivable, and expenses)
* Review and maintain internal policies regarding processes to maintain confidentiality and reduce risks
* Provide technical expertise in the areas of tax, regulations, accounting standards, market practices, trends, and process improvement recommendations
* Ensure all team members are continually developing its market innovation and expert knowledge
* Actively recruit, retain, supervise and develop staff to meet the growing demands of clients
* Implement, manage and create new employee incentive programs such as but not limited to our CPA Reimbursement Program, Education Reimbursement Program, Bonus Incentive Programs, Tax Quarterly Incentives, etc.
* Ensure performance evaluations, staff compensation, goals, and bonus programs are maintained and effectively implemented with continuous review for improvement to ensure the success of team’s development
* Ensure all employees are supervised and managed to perform their functions in strict accord to Company policies and procedures and applicable accounting, income tax rules, regulations, and laws
* Review and assess technology opportunities for efficiency and reporting maximization

**Other Skills**
* Strong technical skills and deep understanding of accounting and tax
* Strong written and verbal communication, presentation, client service, technical writing skills and research skills
* Excellent analytical, organizational, and project management skills
* Ability to manage multiple engagements and competing priorities.
* Strong leadership, business development, recruiting, training, coaching, and mentoring skills, coupled with excellent written, interpersonal, and presentation skills
* Ability to travel as needed to various client sites
* **Travel to outer island offices and California 1 – 2 times annually**

**Preferred Technical Knowledge Areas:**
* Accounting Standards/Generally Accepted Accounting Principals
* U.S. State and Federal rules, regulations, and laws that relate to the preparation of income tax returns for individuals and businesses
* Payroll and Payroll Taxes
* Outsourced CFO and Controller Duties such as but not limited to: organizatio

new_jobs.title: Executive Director - UCAN City Mission
new_jobs.description:
Executive Director \- UCAN City Mission
Reports To: Board of Directors
  

Salary Range: $65,000 – $85,000


Position Summary


The Executive Director serves as the chief executive leader of UCAN City Mission, providing spiritual, strategic, and operational leadership to advance the Mission’s Christ\-centered purpose.


This role sets both short\-term priorities and long\-term vision, ensuring the organization remains focused, sustainable, and responsive to the evolving needs of the community. The Executive Director oversees all aspects of ministry operations, including staff leadership, program effectiveness, financial stewardship, facilities oversight, and community engagement.


As the Mission expands to serve both men and women with children across multiple locations, the Executive Director will guide this growth while maintaining excellence in care, safety, and program outcomes.


The Executive Director leads the Mission through a team of key leaders, equipping and overseeing those responsible for day\-to\-day ministry operations while ensuring strong alignment, accountability, and effectiveness across all areas. The Executive Director remains closely connected to frontline ministry—maintaining visibility with staff and guests and staying informed of the realities, challenges, and opportunities within Mission programs.


This role requires a leader who operates at both the strategic and relational levels—guiding the organization through others while remaining present and connected to those they serve.


Key Responsibilities


Spiritual \& Ministry Leadership


* Model and cultivate a Christ\-centered culture grounded in the Mission’s Statement of Faith
* Provide spiritual leadership to staff and program direction, ensuring ministry is meaningfully integrated into all services
* Support discipleship, restoration, and life transformation through both compassionate care and clear expectations for growth
* Establish a ministry environment that balances grace, accountability, and personal responsibility, recognizing the complexities of addiction, trauma, and mental health
* Ensure all services are delivered in alignment with the Mission’s Christian identity while integrating spiritual formation with program structure to create pathways toward lasting change

Strategic Leadership \& Organizational Direction


* Establish and execute short\-term and long\-term strategic goals aligned with the Mission’s vision
* Lead organizational growth, including expansion to multi\-site operations serving diverse populations
* Monitor performance using defined outcomes and data, adjusting strategy to ensure effectiveness, sustainability, and measurable impact

Staff Leadership, Culture \& Organizational Alignment


* Lead, develop, and support a team of program and operational leaders responsible for day\-to\-day execution
* Establish clear expectations, accountability, and alignment across all departments and locations
* Build and sustain a strong, unified leadership team that reflects the Mission’s values
* Address conflict directly and constructively, strengthening trust and organizational effectiveness
* Ensure leaders are equipped to manage staff and programs with consistency and excellence

Operations, Policy \& Program Oversight


* Provide executive oversight of all Mission operations across multiple sites
* Ensure the development, implementation, and consistent enforcement of policies, procedures, and compliance with applicable laws, regulations, and safety standards
* Hold program leaders accountable for effective, compliant, and mission\-aligned service delivery
* Maintain systems that promote consistency, safety, and organizational discipline
* Maintain a visible and engaged presence within programs to stay connected to frontline realities

Guest Care, Safety \& Program Integrity


* Oversee programs serving individuals experiencing homelessness, addiction, and mental health challenges
* Ensure guests are supported with clear pathways toward stability and success, including appropriate structure and accountability
* Establish and uphold standards that balance grace and responsibility, including when intervention or dismissal is necessary to protect the safety of all guests and staff
* Promote an environment that is safe, respectful, and conducive to transformation

Facilities \& Environment Oversight


* Ensure all facilities are safe, clean, and well\-maintained for both guests and staff
* Oversee maintenance planning and facility operations across current and future locations
* Maintain environments that reflect dignity, order, and care

Financial Management \& Resource Development


* Develop and manage the annual budget in partnership with the Board
* Ensure responsible stewardship and long\-term financial sustainability
* Lead and implement a comprehensive fundraising strategy, including donor engagement, grants, and community partnerships

Board \& Community Relations


* Serve as the primary liaison to the Board of Directors, providing clear and timely reporting on operations, finances, and outcomes
* Support the effectiveness and development of the Board through engagement, planning, and sound governance practices
* Serve as a visible and credible public voice for the Mission, representing its work to churches, donors, community partners, and public stakeholders
* Build and strengthen relationships that advance the Mission’s reach and sustainability, including volunteer engagement aligned with Mission programs

Minimum Qualifications


* Bachelor’s Degree required
* Demonstrated commitment to Jesus Christ, including willingness to affirm and sign the Mission’s Statement of Faith
* Proven experience supervising staff and leading teams
* Strong written and verbal communication skills
* Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite
* Submit to background/criminal history and fingerprint check

Preferred Qualifications \& Competencies


* Experience in nonprofit, ministry, or human services lead

new_jobs.title: Director of Marketing and Communications
new_jobs.description:
**Position Summary**


The Director of Communications provides strategic leadership for the Austen Riggs Center’s communications, marketing, branding, and external engagement. Reporting to the Medical Director/CEO and serving as a member of the senior leadership team, the Director builds and executes an integrated, long\-range communications strategy that advances the Center’s mission, strengthens its national reputation, and supports organizational priorities, including referral development, patient census, philanthropy, education, research, and public engagement.


The Director combines broad knowledge of the national behavioral health care landscape with sophisticated communications judgment. The successful candidate will understand the distinct dynamics of community\-based, nonprofit, academic, independent, investor\-backed treatment organizations and will be able to position Austen Riggs clearly and credibly within this complex and rapidly changing environment.


This is both a strategic and hands\-on leadership role. The Director will navigate a highly collaborative, multidisciplinary workplace; align diverse stakeholders around shared messages and priorities; lead a small communications and marketing team; and strengthen the Center’s digital presence through strong web, search, social, content, analytics, CRM, and responsible AI\-enabled marketing practices.

**Key Responsibilities**

* Develop and lead a comprehensive, multi\-year communications and marketing strategy aligned with the Center’s strategic plan, mission, clinical identity, and institutional priorities.
* Serve as a senior advisor to the Medical Director/CEO, senior leadership, and Board committees on reputation, positioning, executive communications, public affairs, emerging issues, and communications risk.
* Translate the distinctive value of Austen Riggs’ treatment model, institutional history, research, education, and thought leadership into clear, compelling messages for national audiences.
* Maintain a current, nuanced understanding of the behavioral health care marketplace, including private equity activity, nonprofit and community\-based systems, payer and referral trends, workforce pressures, regulatory developments, consumer behavior, and evolving models of care.
* Develop audience\-specific strategies for prospective patients and families, clinicians and referrers, alumni, donors, researchers, educators, policymakers, media, and the broader mental health community.
* Partner closely with Admissions and Referral Relations to strengthen the referral pipeline, improve market intelligence, support census goals, and evaluate the effectiveness of outreach and conversion strategies.
* Collaborate with Development and Alumni Relations, the Erikson Institute, clinical leadership, theOnlineIntensive Outpatient Program, and other departments to ensure that communications are coordinated, mission\-aligned, and mutually reinforcing.
* Lead brand stewardship and ensure consistency, accuracy, accessibility, and quality across all internal and external communications, including publications, presentations, campaigns, events, media relations, and executive messaging.
* Direct the Center’s digital strategy, including website governance and optimization, search engine optimization and marketing, social media, email marketing, content strategy, online reputation, CRM integration, marketing automation, and performance analytics.
* Evaluate and implement responsible uses of artificial intelligence in communications and marketing, including content workflows, audience insights, personalization, search strategy, operational efficiency, quality control, privacy, and brand governance.
* Establish clear goals, dashboards, and key performance indicators; use data to assess reach, engagement, referral activity, conversion, reputation, and return on investment; and present actionable recommendations to senior leadership.
* Lead media relations and serve as an organizational spokesperson, asappropriate, in partnership with the Medical Director/CEO and subject\-matter experts.
* Develop andmaintaincrisis and issues\-management communications plans, ensuringtimely, coordinated,accurate, and values\-based responses.
* Strengthen internal communications so that employees understand organizational priorities, changes, decisions, and the connection between their work and the Center’s mission.
* Lead, mentor, and develop communications and marketing staff; manage external agencies, consultants, freelancers, technology vendors, budgets, and project portfolios.
* Create effective decision\-making and workflow structures across a complex organization, balancing consultation and inclusion with accountability, timeliness, and execution.

**Qualifications**

* Bachelor’s degree in communications, marketing, journalism, public relations, business, health care administration, orrelatedfield; advanced degree preferred.
* At least 10 years of progressively responsible communications and marketing experience, including significant leadership experience in behavioral health, health care, or a closely related mission\-driven field.
* Demonstrated national knowledge of behavioral health carelandscapes includingcommunity, nonprofit, academic, and independent provider settings.
* Proven success leading communications within complex, multidisciplinary organizations thatrequireinfluence across clinical, operational, administrative, governance, and external stakeholder groups.
* Strong record of developing and executing integratedbrand, communications,referral, and growth strategies tied to measurable organizational outcomes.
* Advanced knowledge of digital marketing, website strategy and governance, content management systems, SEO/SEM, social media, email platforms, CRM systems, marketing automation, analytics, and online reputation management.
* Demonstrated ability to assess and apply AI\-enabled tools strategically and responsibly, with attention to accuracy

new_jobs.title: Managing Director, Operations
new_jobs.description:
**Join the Atlas Leadership Team!**
Atlas is a tuition\-free K\-12 charter school that has been committed to providing educational excellence, character development, and meaningful community engagement to our students and families for over 15 years. We are proud to serve our Atlas community of over 92% socioeconomically disadvantaged families and go beyond traditional academics by providing comprehensive family support services that are collectively known as the Atlas Family Scholarship. We know our impactful work would not be possible without our staff and proudly offer a competitive total rewards package, meaningful professional development opportunities, and a culture that ensures our staff feel authentically valued and appreciated for their hard work on a daily basis.


**Our Mission**
Atlas prepares and empowers all students for success on their post\-graduate paths through educational excellence, character development, and community engagement.


**Our Core Values**Connection \| Accountability \| Perseverance \| Growth Mindset*If Atlas aligns with what inspires you each day, we invite you to apply for this meaningful opportunity to serve our southeast Colorado Springs community!*


**What You’ll Do**
Provide strategic leadership and direction for assigned operational departments by establishing clear priorities, monitoring departmental performance, and ensuring alignment with organizational goals. Lead cross\-functional operational initiatives that improve efficiency, effectiveness, and service delivery while fostering collaboration across departments. Coach, develop, and evaluate direct reports, building leadership capacity and accountability throughout the organization. Monitor key performance indicators (KPIs), identify opportunities for continuous improvement, recommend operational enhancements, and provide regular updates to the Chief Executive Officer regarding departmental performance, organizational risks, and progress toward strategic priorities.
*Supervisory Responsibilities** This role will serve as the direct supervisor to the Director of Facilities, Director of Athletics, IT Manager, School Security Manager, and Transportation Manager.

*Cross\-Campus \& Interdepartmental Coordination** Provides strategic coordination across school\-based teams and Operations departments by aligning personnel, resources, schedules, and operational services to ensure the successful delivery of school programs, events, and daily campus operations.

*School Nutrition** Oversee school nutrition operations by partnering with School Food Authority (SFA) to ensure annual meal application completion, and on\-site Food Service Director to ensure high\-quality meal service, compliance, and operational effectiveness.

*Transportation Services** Supervise the Transportation Manager to ensure safe, efficient, and compliant transportation services, including oversight of contracted transportation providers, route effectiveness, and operational performance.

*Facilities \& Custodial Services** Supervise the Director of Facilities to ensure safe, clean, and well\-maintained learning environments through effective facilities management, preventative maintenance, regulatory compliance, and long\-term operational planning.

*IT Department Supervision** Supervise the IT Manager to ensure reliable, secure, and efficient technology systems and services that support student learning, organizational operations, cybersecurity, and long\-term technology planning.

*School Safety** Supervise the School Security Manager to ensure comprehensive school safety systems, emergency preparedness, regulatory compliance, and continuous improvement of campus safety through effective protocols, training, drills, and cross\-functional collaboration.

*School Athletics and After\-School Activities Programming** Supervise the Director of Athletics and Activities to ensure high\-quality, compliant, and sustainable athletic and extracurricular programming that promotes student engagement while coordinating operational support across transportation, facilities, health services, and other departments.

*Facilities Use \& Rentals** Provide strategic oversight of facilities use and rental processes to ensure efficient scheduling, effective coordination, and equitable access for school, athletic, staff, and community events while maximizing operational efficiency and stewardship of school facilities.

**Compensation \& Schedule*** Salaried/Exempt
* Signing Bonus: $3,500, with a 1\-year commitment
* Annual Salary Range: $92,700 \- $110,510 (DOE)
* Full Time; Monday \- Friday; Occasional evenings and weekends as needed




**Benefits*** Approximately 6 weeks of paid school breaks
* 80 hours of annual flex time and 48 hours of annual sick time
* Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision benefits
* Flexible Spending Account or Health Savings Account
* Participation in the Colorado PERA retirement program (required in lieu of social security as a public employee)
* 100% Employer paid short and long\-term disability benefit
* 100% Employer\-paid life insurance benefit of $25,000 (option to purchase more)
* Access to a comprehensive Employee Assistance Program
* Access to thousands of employee discounts

**Annual Calendar \& Start Date*** 230 work days per academic year
* Start Date: August 1, 2026

**Who You Are***Required Qualifications:** Bachelor's degree from an accredited college or university required.
* A minimum of 8 years of relevant experience in K\-12 education, a nonprofit organization, or a similarly complex environment.
* A minimum of 2 years of experience directly supervising employees, including responsibility for coaching, performance management, and professional development.
* Demonstrated experience leading organizational operations, facilities, systems, logistics, and cross\-functional processes, with the ability to develop, implement, and continuously improve operational procedures that support organizational effective

new_jobs.title: Donor Engagement Manager – CEO/CDO Support
new_jobs.description:
**Position: Donor Engagement Manager – CEO/CDO Support**

**About ACE Scholarships**


ACE is a 501(c)(3\) non\-profit, founded in 2000 in Denver, Colorado, to provide low\-income children with equal access to quality K–12 private schools through partial scholarship support. Over the past 25\+ years, ACE has delivered more than 140,000 scholarships worth over $390 million. This school year, ACE is serving approximately 16,000\+ children in Arkansas, Colorado, Delaware, Hawaii, Kansas, Louisiana, Mississippi, Missouri, Montana, New Mexico, Texas, Utah, and Wyoming, and is actively pursuing other expansion opportunities with the support of national funding partners. ACE is actively preparing for the Federal School Choice Tax Credit Program which comes into effect January 2027\. For more information visit the ACE website at www.acescholarships.org.

**Our Mission**  

Our mission is to advance K–12 educational freedom, with a focus on securing financial support for families so they may access the quality education of their choice.

**Our Values:**  

**ACE Founding Principles:** Our founding principles are the foundation of ACE and our North Star. We honor ACE’s founders by remaining committed to these principles, and we passionately advocate for the Mission and Vision these principles support.

**Accountability:** We embrace personal responsibility by taking ownership of our work and focusing on results. We expect both to give and to receive honest feedback.

**Innovation:** We know ideas matter. We prefer the risks and rewards that come with creativity to the certainty that attends stagnation.

**Adaptability:** We position ourselves to take advantage of opportunities, embracing strategic and operational change when it makes our work more impactful.

**Optimism:** We believe our best days are ahead, and we embrace a forward\-thinking mindset. We will make mistakes, but we will remain cheerful in the face of adversity and give people the benefit of the doubt.

**Kindness:** We value everyone on our team, respect differences, and trust one another. This requires courage and strength, as it involves celebrating and giving our undivided attention to others.

**Our Founding Principles:**

* Free enterprise and democracy work
* Parents know the best education environment for their kids
* Private schools offer great value and have open seats
* All people are created equal and deserve an equal chance to pursue the American Dream

**Position Overview**


The **Donor Engagement Manager** is a highly organized, proactive, and warm professional providing high\-level support to the Chief Executive Officer (CEO) and Chief Development Officer (CDO).


The ideal candidate aligns closely with the organization’s values and leadership, communicates effectively, and thrives in a fast\-paced environment. They are responsive, solution\-oriented, and willing to support time\-sensitive needs, including occasional evenings and weekends.


This is a dynamic opportunity for someone eager to grow as a leader. This position also includes occasional travel opportunities to support the CEO and CDO at donor events and strategic engagements.


This role is critical in supporting donor relations, maintaining internal alignment with the Marketing and Development teams, and managing executive\-level logistics with discretion and professionalism.

**Primary Job Responsibilities**

**Donor** **Engagement \& Communication for the CEO and CDO**

* Coordinate donor meetings, major gift planning, and fundraising events
* Maximize follow\-up opportunities from fundraising events by:
* Conducting follow\-up phone calls to schedule one on one donor meetings
* Draft and send thoughtful, professional, and personalized follow\-up notes, emails, and other ACE overview materials
* Draft pre\-meeting notes and meeting confirmations
* Maintain warm, articulate, and professional donor communication
* Help the CEO and CDO stand out by strengthening donor relationships and managing key details
* Send board communications on behalf of the CEO and CDO
* Collaborate with the CEO and CDO to track and fulfill responsibilities tied to annual goals
* Schedule and support weekly prospect meetings with relevant staff
* Coordinate and support webinars and meetings with major gift officers
* Coordinate and support webinars and meetings with major donor prospect, national board and other stakeholders as required

**Donor** **Meeting Preparation**

* Create detailed trip agendas, including donor bios, giving history, and meeting notes
* Prepare briefing documents and materials for donor meetings

**Donor Prospecting**

* Identify influential and wealthy prospects nationally to support diversity of donors across the nation
* Help coordinate meetings, zoom calls with prospects

**Travel \& Event Logistics**

* Coordinate travel arrangements (flights, accommodations, transportation)
* Book restaurants and manage logistics for donor visits and team travel

**Adaptability \& Executive Support**

* Provide direct support to the CEO and CDO for important, time\-sensitive, and occasional last\-minute requests, including projects that require immediate response
* Ensure a calm, efficient, and responsive executive environment
* Understanding that this is a fast\-paced environment, and while we seek to plan as much in advance, circumstances do not always allow for such planning
* Managing CEO and CDO expense reports and monthly review of CEO and CDO budget

**Collaboration with Senior Leadership**

* Partner closely with the President and Marketing Leaders to align on development strategy and execution

**Coordinate National School Choice Partnerships**

* Help CEO and CDO maintain relations with national school choice partners to enhance ACE’s work

**Communications \& Special Projections**

* Collaborate with the communications team on speechwriting, media interviews, and major projects (e.g., organizational magazine)
* Serve as backup for the communications team

new_jobs.title: Executive Assistant to the CEO
new_jobs.description:
**The RMP Promise**


Rocky Mountain Prep is a movement of educators, families, scholars, and advocates committed to changing public education in Colorado by democratizing college access for historically marginalized students and families. We operate twelve college\-preparatory schools serving students from early childhood through twelfth grade, and we are fundamentally a world\-class learning organization that invests deeply in every educator who joins us. Teachers engage in ongoing coaching and professional development, supported by clear expectations, transparent feedback, and opportunities to strengthen their craft and advance along individualized career pathways grounded in our PEAK values. Rooted in a culture of rigor and love, RMP promises to coach, develop, and champion every team member so they can do their best work and grow as professionals, people, and leaders.

**About the Role**


The Executive Assistant to the CEO is Rocky Mountain Prep’s chief anticipator — the person who stays two steps ahead of the CEO, not two steps behind. This is not a traditional administrative role: it is a strategic operations partnership built around one person’s effectiveness, discretion, and pace. The Executive Assistant owns the CEO’s calendar, meeting preparation, and daily operating rhythm; anticipates what the CEO will need before she asks; and treats the unglamorous work of keeping a CEO ready and on track — building agendas, prepping materials, managing logistics — as real, valued operational work, not lesser work. This person is proactive by default: they flag what’s coming, surface what needs attention, and build the systems that keep the CEO’s time aligned to her highest priorities, rather than waiting for a daily task list. This role focuses exclusively on the CEO’s personal readiness and operating rhythm, including her preparation for Board and governance moments — it does not coordinate work across NST teams or own network\-wide logistics, which sit with other functions. The ideal candidate is a strong communicator who can represent the CEO warmly and credibly to any audience, brings genuine professional polish, and is equally comfortable thinking two steps ahead strategically and handling next\-step logistics personally.

 **The Executive Assistant to the CEO at Rocky Mountain Prep will...**

**Proactive Calendar \& Time Stewardship**

* Own and proactively manage the CEO’s calendar, anticipating conflicts, protecting time for driving priorities, and building the week around what matters most before issues arise — not just reacting to requests as they land.
* Coordinate travel, direct\-report check\-ins, and external meetings with an eye toward the CEO’s energy and focus, not just scheduling availability.
* Help the CEO remain accessible and approachable while thoughtfully managing requests on her time — rerouting what can be better handled elsewhere with care and professionalism.

**Meeting \& Agenda Operations**

* Build agendas and prepare the CEO for her standing meetings, 1:1s, and Cabinet check\-ins — arriving at each with a point of view on what needs to be covered, not waiting to be told.
* Facilitate executive meetings by managing time effectively, keeping discussions on track, and driving follow\-up on action items after the CEO’s meetings.
* Anticipate what’s on the horizon — flagging upcoming decisions, deadlines, and moments that need the CEO’s attention before they become urgent.

**Executive Communications \& Materials Support**

* Assist with presentations, briefings, and reports the CEO needs for key engagements, including stakeholder updates and strategic planning sessions.
* Represent the CEO professionally and warmly in day\-to\-day correspondence and scheduling communications with internal and external stakeholders.

**Operations \& Process Excellence**

* Treat the operational and logistical work of supporting a CEO — building systems, tracking details, managing the unglamorous parts of the job — as real, valued strategic work, not administrative overhead.
* Continuously optimize the processes and systems within the Office of the CEO to improve productivity and reduce friction in the CEO’s day\-to\-day operations.
* Manage ad hoc requests and shifting priorities with composure, judgment about what’s urgent versus what can wait, and a bias toward solving problems before they reach the CEO’s desk.

**Guardianship of the CEO’s Time and Well\-being**

* Proactively support the CEO’s sustainable pace, recognizing the importance of balance and resilience in her leadership, and building that awareness into how her calendar and week are constructed.

 **All Teachers at Rocky Mountain Prep will:**


Take ownership of student outcomes by setting high expectations, using data to guide instruction, and ensuring all scholars grow through strong planning, rigorous teaching, and timely re\-teaching. They build inclusive, relationship\-centered classrooms by implementing proactive routines, culturally responsive practices, and consistent communication with families. RMP teachers collaborate closely with colleagues and support staff, contribute to school\-wide initiatives, and support daily operations to maintain a safe and organized environment. They also model RMP’s PEAK values in all interactions and remain flexible and responsive in a fast\-paced, data\-driven environment aligned to our evolving Concept Essence.

**Required**:

* Demonstrated ability to work proactively and anticipate needs — this role is not a fit for someone who prefers to be told exactly what to do each day.
* Experience operating as a strategic partner to a senior leader, not a traditional administrative support role; comfortable with both big\-picture anticipation and hands\-on logistics in the same day.
* Exceptional organizational skills and follow\-through, with the ability to manage a fast\-moving, high\-stakes calendar without dropping details.
* Strong written and verbal communication skills, wi

new_jobs.title: Chief Operating Officer
new_jobs.description:
This position is incentive eligible.  

  

  

##### **Introduction**


Executives thrive with us! HCA Healthcare is one of the nation’s leading providers of healthcare services, comprising of over 180 hospitals and about 2,000 sites of care in 21 states and the United Kingdom. We are looking for a Chief Operating Officer for our HCA Houston Healthcare Southeast team where excellence creates excellence.

##### **Benefits**


HCA Houston Healthcare Southeast, offers a total rewards package that supports the health, life, career and retirement of our colleagues. The available plans and programs include:

* Comprehensive medical coverage that covers many common services at no cost or for a low copay. Plans include prescription drug and behavioral health coverage as well as free telemedicine services and free AirMed medical transportation.
* Additional options for dental and vision benefits, life and disability coverage, flexible spending accounts, supplemental health protection plans (accident, critical illness, hospital indemnity), auto and home insurance, identity theft protection, legal counseling, long\-term care coverage, moving assistance, pet insurance and more.
* Free counseling services and resources for emotional, physical and financial wellbeing
* 401(k) Plan with a 100% match on 3% to 9% of pay (based on years of service)
* Employee Stock Purchase Plan with 10% off HCA Healthcare stock
* Family support through fertility and family building benefits with Progyny and adoption assistance.
* Referral services for child, elder and pet care, home and auto repair, event planning and more
* Consumer discounts through Abenity and Consumer Discounts
* Retirement readiness, rollover assistance services and preferred banking partnerships
* Education assistance (tuition, student loan, certification support, dependent scholarships)
* Colleague recognition program
* Time Away From Work Program (paid time off, paid family leave, long\- and short\-term disability coverage and leaves of absence)
* Employee Health Assistance Fund that offers free employee\-only coverage to full\-time and part\-time colleagues based on income.


Learn more about Employee Benefits

***Note: Eligibility for benefits may vary by location.***

  

HCA Healthcare has expanded our influence across the healthcare industry by investing $3\.5 billion in capital improvements in recent years. Do you want to be an influencer in healthcare? **Apply for our Chief Operating Officer role today!**

##### **Job Summary and Qualifications**

  

The role of the Chief Operating Officer (COO) assumes line responsibility and authority for the administrative direction, evaluation, and coordination of the functions and activities of assigned departments within the hospital organization to ensure operation objectives and results are in accord with overall hospital needs. In the absence of the hospital, CEO and/or as assigned, represents the CEO in the coordination of entire portions of the hospital organization, speaking and acting within the scope of objectives set forth in the practice and/or policy of the hospital.

  

What you will do in this role:

* You will function at an executive level in an active leadership role with the hospital’s governing body, medical staff leadership, hospital senior leadership and management team
* You will be directly responsible for the improvement of the hospitals facilities in assigned areas, including construction or renovation of structures and the purchasing of new equipment
* You will be responsible for department leaders in establishing a measure of performance, increase productivity, quality improvement, cost controls and efficient utilization of facilities
* You will ensure the consistent and effective execution of key systems and processes that make effective use of organizational resources
* You will lead a team or unit to enhance product or service quality; driving the business toward enhanced product or service quality
* You will create a work environment in which employees committed to their organization and feel pride and job ownership
* You will build strategic alliances and partnerships within the organization to collaboratively execute business strategies
* You will create an environment in which products and processes are designed to ensure customer satisfaction; effectively incorporating customer perspectives in all business activities
* You will contribute to the development of the organization’s strategic goals and objectives as well as the overall management of the organization
* You will promote consistent positive patient interactions that advance the agenda of unparalleled patient service
* You will perform other duties as assigned

  

What qualifications you will need:

* Master’s degree in Business Administration, Healthcare Administration, or related field required
* 3\+ years of experience in progressive acute care management and refined management skills as a COO or through a COO development program or similar executive level experience required

**HCA Houston Healthcare Southeast** is a 391 \- bed acute care facility that has been serving the needs of southeast Harris County for over 45 years. With over 250 physicians on staff specializing in all areas of medicine, and over 1000 employees working to provide the highest level of care the hospital has retained its community hospital atmosphere while attaining state of the art technology.

**HCA Healthcare (Corporate)**, based in Nashville, Tennessee, supports a variety of corporate roles from business operations to administrative positions. Like our colleagues in any HCA Healthcare hospital, our corporate campus employees enjoy unparalleled **resources and opportunities** to reach their potential as healthcare leaders and innovators. From market rate compensation to continuing education and **career advancement opportunities**, every person has a solid foundation for success. Nashville is also home to our **Executive Deve

new_jobs.title: President/CEO
new_jobs.description:
Description:
**Why Seamen’s Bank, and Why Now?**


Headquartered on the Outer Cape in Provincetown, Massachusetts and with approximately $500M in assets, Seamen’s Bank just celebrated its 175th birthday. While we honor our past that is rich with history and success, we now look to our future with confidence and ambition. We are focused on strategically advancing our financial performance, widening and strengthening our customer base, and increasing our community impact.


Seamen’s Bank is seeking an experienced financial executive for the position of President \& CEO to propel the Bank into its next stage of growth and development. This top executive will be responsible for the Bank’s overall strategic direction, financial performance, and regulatory compliance. As Seamen’s Bank is a community bank, the incoming leader will need to carefully balance maximizing profitability and asset growth with maintaining deep local relationships and serving the economic needs of the community.

**MAJOR RESPONSIBILITIES \& ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS**

**Leadership, Strategic Vision, \& Execution:** In collaboration with the Board and the senior management team, develops and executes the long\-term vision for the Bank and effectively communicates the vision and strategic planning throughout the Bank.

* Oversee the development and implementation of the Bank’s strategic plans and objectives to ensure financial growth and stability.
* Brings a thoughtful and common\-sense approach to the goals and priorities of the Bank while adhering to the Bank’s mission and values.
* Guides and supports the management team in operational decisions and policy development; active on various internal and external committees.
* Directly manages, supports, and evaluates the performance of the senior management team.
* Able to anticipate and identify developing issues facing the Bank. Proactively and strategically plans to address these issues.
* Serves as a member of the Board of Trustees and is the chief liaison between the Board and members of management.
* Drive and encourage discussions on decision\-making surrounding policies that are established in the best long\-term interests of the depositors, staff, and community.
* Ability to make critical, difficult, and timely decisions, then execute and follow through to completion and delivery.
* Demonstrated ability to shape and steer positive organizational culture and staff engagement.
* Upholding an environment that encourages open communication, transparency, and a healthy debate of ideas. Champions change and innovation without instilling fear.

**Business Performance \& Operations:** The President \& CEO will partner with senior management to lead and develop an organization of 75 staff to achieve strategic financial and operational goals. This future leader will ensure the smooth running of the Bank, eliminate ineffective practices, and embrace innovation and forward thinking.

* Fosters a culture of excellence, ensuring strong business performance based on innovation, minimizing duplication and waste, and maximizing efficiencies.
* Monitors balance sheet strategies, investment portfolio, and capital portfolio and expenditures.
* Provides exemplary stewardship of Bank resources.
* Demonstrates strong familiarity with technologies to support business and financial functions.
* Monitors overall financial performance and oversees decisions to drive profitability and resource allocation to steer the Bank towards its goals.
* Continual review and improvement of all Bank operations, including lending, deposits, credit, other financial services, safety, and security.

**Community Relations \& Development:** Seamen’s Bank is a solid, long\-time financial partner to Outer Cape organizations and depositors. The Bank is a leader largely due to its continued dedication to collaboration and to making impactful improvements to the local economy. To this end, the President \& CEO must be both an engaging executive and an inspiring community leader.

* Will serve as the primary community ambassador of the Bank with a history of community engagement.
* Leadership and/or guidance on the activities of the Seamen’s Bank charitable foundation.
* Agile in building and strengthening relationships with customers, business leaders, government officials, and community organizations to improve the Bank’s reputation and establish a wider market presence.
* Is an articulate and visible public speaker who can communicate with a diverse audience on a broad range of pressing local issues.
* A genuine interest in impacting the local economy.

**Risk Identification \& Mitigation:** Seamen’s Bank strives for the highest level of compliance and thoughtfully accepts a level of risk aligned with the best interest of the depositors.

* A required aptitude for risk management and identifying potential issues at the Bank, within the industry, and potential impact on the community. Develops strategies and initiatives to mitigate those risks.
* Maintains an extensive, comprehensive understanding of state and federal banking laws and regulatory requirements to successfully and effectively steer the Bank through regulatory examinations and audits.
* Ensures the Bank complies with federal, state, and local regulations, maintaining ethical standards and risk management practices.
* Monitors all activities of the Bank including new loan requests, overdrafts, and exceptions that elevate financial risk.
* Leads the Bank through financial or operational challenges, ensuring calm, effective decision\-making and clear and transparent communication during crises.
* Engages complex Bank matters to legal counsel and ensures best outcomes for the Bank.

**ADDITIONAL RESPONSIBILITIES**

* Regularly communicates, advises, and collaborates with the Board regarding strategic direction, ongoing operations state, arising issues, and policy.
* Facilitates cross\-departmental efforts to maximize efficiencies and eliminates duplication an

new_jobs.company: Boys & Girls Club of Metropolitan Baltimore
new_jobs.title: Chief Development Officer
new_jobs.description:
**Chief Development Officer**
**Department:** Executive
**Reports to:** COO
**Hours:** Full\-time
**Location:** Administrative Office (1201 S. Sharp St., Suite 302, Baltimore, MD 21230\)
**Salary Range:** $130,000 – $160,000
**FLSA Status:** Exempt
**POSITION SUMMARY:**The Chief Development Officer is the organization’s senior leader responsible for all contributed revenue and the systems, people, and strategy required to achieve fundraising goals. The CDO serves as a key partner to the CEO and COO, ensuring that fundraising, donor engagement, and impact storytelling are aligned, compelling, and results‑driven.  

**POSITION RESPONSIBILITIES:**
Revenue Strategy \& Results
* Own and achieve annual and multi\-year contributed revenue goals that sustain a \~$6M, primarily philanthropy\-funded organization
* Develop and execute comprehensive fundraising strategies across: Major gifts, Institutional giving (grants, foundations, public funding), Annual giving and campaigns, and Events
* Monitor revenue performance and adjust strategies as needed
* Build and manage a healthy donor pipeline at all levels

Donor \& Funder Relationships
* Personally manage a portfolio of top individual donors and funders
* Partner with the CEO on cultivation, solicitation, and stewardship of major donors
* Ensure high‑quality stewardship and reporting for all donors and funders
* Serve as a key external ambassador for the organization’s impact and mission

Communications \& Storytelling
* Partner closely with Communications to align messaging, brand, and narrative strategy with fundraising priorities
* Collaborate to ensure consistent, donor\-centered storytelling across all channels
* Co\-develop communications campaigns that support revenue goals
* Work together to translate program data and outcomes into compelling donor narratives
* Maintain a strong public presence in collaboration with the Communications function to support revenue generation

Team Leadership \& Capacity Building
* Lead, coach, and develop the Development team
* Set clear expectations, goals, and metrics for direct reports
* Build a culture of accountability, collaboration, and donor\-centered fundraising
* Ensure systems, tools, and processes are in place to support growth

Cross‑Functional Leadership
* Partner with Programs and Data \& Impact to align funding priorities with outcomes
* Collaborate with Finance to ensure grant compliance and revenue forecasting
* Work with executive leadership to align fundraising strategy with organizational priorities
* Participate as a core member of the EOS Leadership Team


  

**SKILLS/KNOWLEDGE REQUIRED:**
* Demonstrated success leading fundraising for a multi\-million\-dollar, philanthropy\-driven organization (approximately $4M–$10M), with consistent achievement of ambitious revenue goals
* Proven track record of personally closing major gifts (typically $25K–$250K\+) and managing a high\-value donor portfolio
* Experience operating in resource\-constrained, growth\-oriented environments, with the ability to balance strategic leadership and hands\-on execution
* Strong executive relationship‑building skills, with experience partnering with CEOs and boards to cultivate and solicit donors
* Demonstrated ability to lead, develop, and hold accountable high‑performing Development teams

**Disclaimer:**The information presented indicates the general nature and level of work expected of employees in this classification. It is not designed to contain, nor to be interpreted as, a comprehensive inventory of all duties, responsibilities, qualifications, and objectives required of employees assigned to this job. Further, no part of this description shall be construed as a contract or guarantee of employment. All BGCMB employees are at\-will employees. BGCMB is an Equal Employment Opportunity Employer

new_jobs.title: Chief Executive Officer
new_jobs.description:
Chief Executive Officer (CEO) \& Chief Artificial Intelligence Transformation OfficerJaweed Enterprise (JE)

**Position Title:** Chief Executive Officer (CEO) \& Chief Artificial Intelligence Transformation Officer

**Reports To:** Board of Directors

**Direct Reports:** Program Directors, Business Managers, Chief Financial Officer, Chief Technology Officer, AI Program Managers, Operations Managers, Portfolio Company Leadership Teams, and other executive personnel as assigned.

Executive Summary

The Chief Executive Officer (CEO) serves as the highest\-ranking executive officer of Jaweed Enterprise, providing strategic leadership, operational oversight, financial stewardship, technological innovation, and long\-term vision for the organization and its portfolio companies. The CEO is responsible for driving organizational growth, maximizing shareholder value, expanding market opportunities, evaluating acquisitions, developing leaders, and ensuring operational excellence across all business units.

As the principal executive of JE, the CEO operates at the intersection of business strategy, investment management, artificial intelligence, digital transformation, organizational development, and stakeholder engagement. The CEO will work closely with the Board of Directors to develop and execute strategic initiatives that position JE as a leader in business incubation, revitalization, acquisition, restructuring, and long\-term enterprise growth.

Additionally, the CEO will oversee the strategic deployment of Artificial Intelligence (AI), Generative AI, Machine Learning (ML), Robotic Process Automation (RPA), Predictive Analytics, and other emerging technologies throughout JE and its portfolio companies.

Primary Responsibilities1\. Strategic Leadership

The CEO shall:

* Develop and execute long\-term strategic plans.
* Align business objectives with shareholder interests.
* Identify new growth opportunities, acquisitions, partnerships, and investments.
* Evaluate industry trends and emerging market opportunities.
* Drive innovation throughout the organization.
* Develop and maintain competitive advantages across all business sectors.
* Lead business expansion efforts both domestically and internationally.
* Establish strategic objectives and measurable performance benchmarks.
* Monitor and adjust strategic initiatives as market conditions evolve.
* Ensure organizational sustainability while maximizing enterprise value.

2\. Artificial Intelligence Leadership \& Innovation

The CEO will serve as the chief advocate and architect of AI transformation throughout Jaweed Enterprise.

AI Strategic Responsibilities

The CEO shall:

* Develop and implement an enterprise\-wide AI strategy.
* Integrate Artificial Intelligence into all organizational functions.
* Identify opportunities where AI can improve revenue, efficiency, profitability, and customer experience.
* Establish AI governance frameworks ensuring ethical and responsible AI deployment.
* Promote AI adoption throughout all portfolio companies.
* Evaluate AI\-related investment opportunities.
* Build AI competency at all levels of the organization.

Generative AI Leadership

The CEO will:

* Champion the use of Generative AI technologies.
* Incorporate AI assistants into executive decision\-making.
* Utilize AI for strategic planning and forecasting.
* Oversee the deployment of Large Language Models (LLMs).
* Implement AI\-powered research, reporting, and analysis systems.
* Drive AI\-assisted innovation initiatives.
* Ensure AI solutions align with organizational objectives.

AI Business Applications

The CEO will oversee AI implementation across:

* Finance
* Marketing
* Sales
* Operations
* Human Resources
* Legal Services
* Customer Support
* Portfolio Management
* Risk Management
* Business Development
* Investor Relations
* Supply Chain Operations

Emerging Technology Oversight

The CEO will maintain active knowledge of:

* Generative AI
* Large Language Models
* Machine Learning
* Deep Learning
* Predictive Analytics
* Intelligent Automation
* Robotics
* Blockchain
* Cybersecurity
* Digital Twins
* Quantum Computing
* Data Analytics
* Cloud Computing Platforms

3\. Acquisition, Incubation \& Business Transformation

As JE specializes in identifying, acquiring, repairing, and scaling businesses, the CEO shall:

* Source acquisition opportunities.
* Conduct strategic due diligence.
* Lead mergers and acquisitions.
* Evaluate distressed business opportunities.
* Develop turnaround strategies.
* Drive operational restructuring efforts.
* Establish post\-acquisition integration plans.
* Evaluate technology modernization opportunities.
* Using AI\-driven market intelligence, identify undervalued companies.
* Create growth strategies for incubated businesses.
* Maximize enterprise valuation prior to divestitures or public offerings.

4\. Operational Leadership

The CEO shall:

* Direct day\-to\-day operations.
* Ensure operational excellence across all business units.
* Establish performance measurement systems.
* Improve workflows and processes.
* Optimize organizational efficiencies.
* Implement quality assurance standards.
* Monitor organizational productivity.
* Reduce waste and inefficiencies using data\-driven methodologies.
* Lead organizational transformation initiatives.
* Promote a culture of accountability and execution.

5\. Financial Leadership \& Capital Management

The CEO shall:

* Oversee financial planning and budgeting.
* Monitor organizational financial performance.
* Present financial reports to shareholders and the Board.
* Approve major expenditures and investments.
* Manage capital allocation.
* Ensure profitability targets are achieved.
* Lead fundraising efforts.
* Develop financing strategies.
* Evaluate investment opportunities.
* Maximize shareholder returns.
* Guide IPO, Reverse IPO, SPAC, and public market strategies where appropriate.

AI\-Driven Financial Management

The CEO shall:

* Utilize AI\-powered foreca

The Chief Executive Officer (CEO) serves as the highest\-ranking executive officer of Jaweed Enterprise, providing strategic leadership, operational oversight, financial stewardship, technological innovation, and long\-term vision for the organization and its portfolio companies. The CEO is responsible for driving organizational growth, maximizing shareholder value, expanding market opportunities, evaluating acquisitions, developing leaders, and ensuring operational excellence across all business units.

As the principal executive of JE, the CEO operates at the intersection of business strategy, investment management, artificial intelligence, digital transformation, organizational development, and stakeholder engagement. The CEO will work closely with the Board of Directors to develop and execute strategic initiatives that position JE as a leader in business incubation, revitalization, acquisition, restructuring, and long\-term enterprise growth.

Additionally, the CEO will oversee the strategic deployment of Artificial Intelligence (AI), Generative AI, Machine Learning (ML), Robotic Process Automation (RPA), Predictive Analytics, and other emerging technologies throughout JE and its portfolio companies.

Primary Responsibilities1\. Strategic Leadership

The CEO shall:

* Develop and execute long\-term strategic plans.
* Align business objectives with shareholder interests.
* Identify new growth opportunities, acquisitions, partnerships, and investments.
* Evaluate industry trends and emerging market opportunities.
* Drive innovation throughout the organization.
* Develop and maintain competitive advantages across all business sectors.
* Lead business expansion efforts both domestically and internationally.
* Establish strategic objectives and measurable performance benchmarks.
* Monitor and adjust strategic initiatives as market conditions evolve.
* Ensure organizational sustainability while maximizing enterprise value.

2\. Artificial Intelligence Leadership \& Innovation

The CEO will serve as the chief advocate and architect of AI transformation throughout Jaweed Enterprise.

AI Strategic Responsibilities

The CEO shall:

* Develop and implement an enterprise\-wide AI strategy.
* Integrate Artificial Intelligence into all organizational functions.
* Identify opportunities where AI can improve revenue, efficiency, profitability, and customer experience.
* Establish AI governance frameworks ensuring ethical and responsible AI deployment.
* Promote AI adoption throughout all portfolio companies.
* Evaluate AI\-related investment opportunities.
* Build AI competency at all levels of the organization.

Generative AI Leadership

The CEO will:

* Champion the use of Generative AI technologies.
* Incorporate AI assistants into executive decision\-making.
* Utilize AI for strategic planning and forecasting.
* Oversee the deployment of Large Language Models (LLMs).
* Implement AI\-powered research, reporting, and analysis systems.
* Drive AI\-assisted innovation initiatives.
* Ensure AI solutions align with organizational objectives.

AI Business Applications

The CEO will oversee AI implementation across:

* Finance
* Marketing
* Sales
* Operations
* Human Resources
* Legal Services
* Customer Support
* Portfolio Management
* Risk Management
* Business Development
* Investor Relations
* Supply Chain Operations

Emerging Technology Oversight

The CEO will maintain active knowledge of:

* Generative AI
* Large Language Models
* Machine Learning
* Deep Learning
* Predictive Analytics
* Intelligent Automation
* Robotics
* Blockchain
* Cybersecurity
* Digital Twins
* Quantum Computing
* Data Analytics
* Cloud Computing Platforms

3\. Acquisition, Incubation \& Business Transformation

As JE specializes in identifying, acquiring, repairing, and scaling businesses, the CEO shall:

* Source acquisition opportunities.
* Conduct strategic due diligence.
* Lead mergers and acquisitions.
* Evaluate distressed business opportunities.
* Develop turnaround strategies.
* Drive operational restructuring efforts.
* Establish post\-acquisition integration plans.
* Evaluate technology modernization opportunities.
* Using AI\-driven market intelligence, identify undervalued companies.
* Create growth strategies for incubated businesses.
* Maximize enterprise valuation prior to divestitures or public offerings.

4\. Operational Leadership

The CEO shall:

* Direct day\-to\-day operations.
* Ensure operational excellence across all business units.
* Establish performance measurement systems.
* Improve workflows and processes.
* Optimize organizational efficiencies.
* Implement quality assurance standards.
* Monitor organizational productivity.
* Reduce waste and inefficiencies using data\-driven methodologies.
* Lead organizational transformation initiatives.
* Promote a culture of accountability and execution.

5\. Financial Leadership \& Capital Management

The CEO shall:

* Oversee financial planning and budgeting.
* Monitor organizational financial performance.
* Present financial reports to shareholders and the Board.
* Approve major expenditures and investments.
* Manage capital allocation.
* Ensure profitability targets are achieved.
* Lead fundraising efforts.
* Develop financing strategies.
* Evaluate investment opportunities.
* Maximize shareholder returns.
* Guide IPO, Reverse IPO, SPAC, and public market strategies where appropriate.

AI\-Driven Financial Management

new_jobs.title: Director of Philanthropy
new_jobs.description:
**General Description:**


This position leads and oversees a major portion of the Food Bank’s fundraising effort. The primary objective is to lead the Philanthropy Team in effectively planning, developing, and implementing a fundraising plan for major donors (ind, corps, orgs) including, but not limited to, the identification, cultivation, solicitation, recognition, and tracking of such donors. The Director of Philanthropy oversees several staff members.




**Essential Functions:**

* Prepare and execute a comprehensive fundraising plan to engage major donors that meets planned expectations and goals of the organization, as determined by the CEO and CDO.
* Working closely with the CDO to design and implement a strategic vision for SAFB to identify, solicit and cultivate relationships with major donors.
* Lead the strategic oversight, philanthropy goals setting and management of the SAFB philanthropy department including new and established portfolios, signature events, third party events, seasonal fundraising campaigns, food and fund drives.
* Lead/Assist in the preparation of written materials, including correspondence, solicitation materials, acknowledgements, and regular communications for major gifts donors.
* Lead, motivate, and develop a high functioning team; hire, train, mentor, supervise, and evaluate staff of Philanthropy Managers, Special Events Manager, Food and Fund Drive Coordinator, and Interfaith Engagement Manager.
* Monitor and report key metrics, generate reports, and make recommendations to enhance philanthropy performance.
* Manage the philanthropy team in the use of Salesforce daily.
* Collaborate with the Development teams to staff donor events, meet and greet with major giving donors and prospects.
* Serve as liaison to donors, prospects, and partners, including representing Food Bank at national, regional, and local meetings as well as engage in face\-to\-face tours and presentations.
* Build strong internal cross\-departmental relationships across the Food Bank.
* Advocate internally for policies and procedures that enhance donor experience.
* Comply with all policies and guidelines and accept additional duties as assigned by the CDO.

**Qualifications:**

* Bachelor’s degree in business, marketing, communications, or a relevant field or equivalent related work experience. Master’s or Certifications preferred.
* Progressive experience (5\+ years) in fund development.
* Proven success in securing 6 figure major gifts from individuals, corporations, or foundations.
* Minimum of 3 years supervisory experience of full\-time paid development staff and leading teams.
* Strength in hiring, recruiting, managing, developing, coaching, and retaining individuals and teams.
* Demonstrated ability to supervise others and delegate appropriate tasks.
* Exceptional emotional intelligence to connect with
* Proficient using Microsoft Office products including Outlook, Word, and advanced Excel skills.
* Expert\-level knowledge in CRM or database management. Salesforce preferred.
* Knowledgeable in budget management, forecasting, and financial modeling of fundraising goals.
* Proven ethical fundraising standards, good judgment, and discretion.
* Ability to work independently with minimum supervision.
* Ability to organize work, time, and self.
* Orientation to detail, accuracy, and the meeting of deadlines.
* Ability to collaborate, advise, and influence anyone from staff to community leaders.
* Superior presentation skills.
* Ability to present a professional demeanor under a variety of conditions.
* Night and weekend work is required and routine.
* Ability to travel as needed; reliable transportation required.
* Possession of a current Texas driver's license, liability insurance and a clean driving record.

**Physical Demands**


The work environment characteristics described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions and responsibilities of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.


While performing the duties of this job, the employee is constantly required to sit, talk, hear, keyboard, and utilize fine manipulation and near visual acuity. The employee is occasionally required to walk, utilize gross manipulation, push, pull, reach, and stoop. The employee must occasionally lift, and/or move up to 25 pounds.


**Work Environment**


The above statements are intended to describe the general nature and levels of work to be performed and are not intended to be an exhaustive list of all responsibilities and duties.

new_jobs.title: Executive Director, Head of Business Growth & Entrepreneurship, Global Philanthropy
new_jobs.description:
**JOB DESCRIPTION**  

  

  

Ready to drive inclusive economic growth at scale? You will lead a high\-performing team and cross\-sector partnerships that support underserved entrepreneurs and small businesses in building, sustaining, and scaling their ventures. You will sharpen your strategic leadership and expand your influence across a mission\-driven, global organization. Join us to mobilize capital, accelerate innovation, and help more entrepreneurs build wealth, resilience, and long\-term opportunity.




As an Executive Director, Head of Business Growth \& Entrepreneurship in U.S. Philanthropy, you will lead a high\-impact portfolio focused on expanding access to resources, capital, and opportunities for underserved small businesses and entrepreneurs. You will lead the firm's philanthropy strategy to foster business creation, growth, and sustainability by increasing access to capital, strengthening entrepreneurial ecosystems, and supporting pathways to long\-term business success—particularly for businesses that have historically faced barriers to growth.




You will manage a team that designs and executes major initiatives and partners with government, philanthropic, nonprofit, corporate, industry, and community leaders to deliver innovative solutions at scale. Your responsibilities include strategy development and analysis, strategic grantmaking and program design, and thought leadership across a large, multifaceted organization. You'll collaborate with JPMorgan Chase's most senior executives to evaluate market trends, policies, and ecosystem gaps, assess implications for the firm and the communities we serve, and shape strategies that advance inclusive economic growth and opportunity.




The Corporate Responsibility team at JPMorgan Chase advances a stronger, more inclusive economy and society for clients, customers, and communities globally. The Global Philanthropy team consists of Employee Engagement \& Volunteerism, Impact Finance, and grantmaking teams at the national and local levels. The Business Growth \& Entrepreneurship team's core functions are to develop and execute strategies that support underserved small businesses, evaluate and manage grant portfolios, and provide thought leadership on inclusive entrepreneurship, small business growth, and economic mobility.




**Job Responsibilities**

* Design and lead major initiatives that expand access to capital, improve the efficiency and scale of support systems for underserved small businesses, and strengthen outcomes for entrepreneurs across growth stages
* Lead, develop, and mentor a high\-performing team of program officers and associates
* In collaboration with business leaders, design, evaluate, and lead execution of strategic entrepreneurship initiatives and identify where JPMC products and services can drive complementary impact for underserved business owners
* Partner with Corporate Responsibility leaders, government agencies, industry stakeholders, and nonprofit organizations to inform policies that support inclusive business growth and entrepreneurship
* Establish portfolio priorities and make strategic recommendations regarding investments, partnerships, and resource allocation, balancing impact opportunities, organizational capacity, and risk considerations
* Serve as lead relationship manager with key external partners and leaders across the firm
* Maintain strong governance, risk management, and grantmaking controls across the portfolio, ensuring responsible stewardship of philanthropic resources and compliance with firm standards and Corporate Responsibility procedures
* Monitor external environment for economic trends, ecosystem gaps, and partnership opportunities related to small business growth; share insights with key line of business partners
* Serve as a trusted advisor and representative of the firm on issues related to underserved small business growth, access to capital, and inclusive entrepreneurship with external partners, policymakers, and industry stakeholders




**Required Qualifications, Capabilities, and Skills**

* Bachelor's degree
* 15\+ years of experience in small business development, entrepreneurship, or inclusive economic opportunity initiatives, including leading large\-scale programs
* Deep understanding of financial products and services that support underserved small businesses (e.g., access to credit, capital solutions, CDFIs, technical assistance)
* Demonstrated ability to influence senior stakeholders and drive results across a complex matrix organization
* Experience managing large\-scale portfolios and making strategic resource allocation decisions
* Experience leading high\-performing teams, with a strong commitment to coaching, mentorship, and talent development
* Track record of successful partnerships that expand opportunity and access for underserved small businesses
* Experience working in a fast\-paced, collaborative, cross\-functional environment
* Exceptional oral and written communication skills




**Preferred Qualifications, Capabilities, and Skills**

* Recognized thought leadership in small business growth, inclusive entrepreneurship, or economic mobility
* Strong judgment, risk assessment, and decision\-making capabilities
* Outstanding analytical and problem\-solving skills
* Strong project management skills with the ability to deliver results within demanding timelines


**ABOUT US**  

  

  

JPMorganChase, one of the oldest financial institutions, offers innovative financial solutions to millions of consumers, small businesses and many of the world's most prominent corporate, institutional and government clients under the J.P. Morgan and Chase brands. Our history spans over 200 years and today we are a leader in investment banking, consumer and small business banking, commercial banking, financial transaction processing and asset management.
We offer a competitive total rewards package including base salary determined based on the role, experience,

new_jobs.title: Federal Government Relations - Executive Director
new_jobs.description:
**JOB DESCRIPTION**  

  

  

Global Government Relations (GGR) is housed within Corporate Responsibility and is responsible for developing and implementing a comprehensive policy, regulatory and advocacy strategy on behalf of JPMorgan Chase. This includes engaging in balanced advocacy with regulators and policymakers, understanding and providing thought leadership on how policies affect the firm's clients and economic growth overall, and governing internal coordination among regions and lines of business on global regulatory reform issues.


As an Executive Director within the Federal Government Relations (FGR) team, you will be working in collaboration with the Global Government Relations Team to oversee legislative activities, scrutinize policy and political trends, and enlighten Congressional and Executive Branch public officials on matters of mutual interest. Your responsibilities will also include closely liaising with the firm's business lines and clients to comprehend emerging issues, and to devise, support, and advocate for the progression of public policy priorities that influence the firm and our clients.  



**Job responsibilities**

* Own a portfolio of priority federal legislative issues, setting advocacy strategy and driving end\-to\-end execution of legislative and public policy initiatives to advance firm objectives.
* Serve as a trusted advisor to JPMorganChase senior leadership, aligning stakeholders on policy priorities, tradeoffs, and engagement plans to ensure a unified firm position.
* Build and sustain high\-impact relationships with federal policymakers across Congress and the Executive Branch, enhancing access, credibility, and influence on priority issues.
* Develop and lead multi\-stakeholder advocacy efforts with industry and external partners; provide strategic direction to trade associations to maximize effectiveness and outcomes.
* Design and execute targeted advocacy campaigns to advance the firm's priorities with federal policymakers, integrating messaging, outreach cadence, and stakeholder mapping.
* Produce executive\-ready briefing materials (memos, talking points, and presentations) that translate complex policy developments into clear business implications and recommended actions.

Lead cross\-functional working teams, driving governance, accountability, and on\-time delivery across policy initiatives.  
* 

**Required qualifications, capabilities, and skills:**

* 12\+ years of experience in policy and/or legislative development and analysis
* Deep understanding of capital markets, financial services, and business issues
* Demonstrated leadership skills, strategic thinking, policy acumen, and strong work ethic
* Proven and effective lobbying outcomes at the federal level
* Exceptional relationships and considerable experience with navigating and understanding federal politics and legislative processes
* Demonstrated excellent written and verbal communications skills, including both public speaking and the ability to engage with senior executives

**Preferred qualifications, capabilities, and skills:**

* Strong project management and organizational skills
* Ability to excel and maintain a positive attitude in a high\-performance, fast\-paced professional environment, while managing multiple projects under tight deadlines
* Management and leadership experience is a plus
* JD or MBA is a plus




**ABOUT US**  

  

  

JPMorganChase, one of the oldest financial institutions, offers innovative financial solutions to millions of consumers, small businesses and many of the world's most prominent corporate, institutional and government clients under the J.P. Morgan and Chase brands. Our history spans over 200 years and today we are a leader in investment banking, consumer and small business banking, commercial banking, financial transaction processing and asset management.
